_README: MmapGen per-map / per-tile generator config. Top-level keys are stringified map IDs; values for that map are flat scalars (agentRadius, agentHeight, walkableRadius, walkableHeight, walkableSlopeAngle, etc.). Per-tile overrides go in nested objects under the map (the inner key is `tileX || tileY`, no separator). The `default` block at the top is read by tools/NavDataAudit only -- MmapGen itself ignores it. See docs/physics/MMAP_FORMAT.md.
_agentNotes: Tauren Male is the largest WoW capsule (radius 1.0247, height 2.625). All continent maps must be generated for that capsule. agentRadius / agentHeight are in WoW meters. walkableRadius/walkableHeight/walkableClimb were previously specified as integer cell counts tied to ch=0.25 / cs=0.2666; that is a footgun when ch or cs change (PFS-OVERHAUL-006: setting them to 0 lets TileWorker auto-derive cell counts from agentRadius/agentHeight/agentMaxClimbModelTerrainTransition divided by cs/ch, producing correct world-unit clearance / climb regardless of cell size). Per-tile walkableErosionRadius is a separate source-support radius for rcErodeWalkableArea only; it does not change the Detour/header agent capsule.

0._3345_README: BRD/BRM route tile for Flame Crest -> BRD approach / upper-spire portal cluster. Apply only source-support erosion tuning here; do not lower climb or add route repair.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 BRM Round-4-redo (2026-05-09): tested per-tile agentMaxClimbTerrain overrides for tile filename 0004634.mmtile (CLI 34,46) at values 1.0 and 0.2. 1.0 had negligible effect (Walk count 299->296). 0.2 was destructive: Walk count dropped to 13/1071 because Detour rerouted around the over-fragmented tile via a worse cliff corridor. The OG-zeppelin Cycle-17e fix worked because the over-collapsed polygon was a shortcut; disconnecting it forced the bot onto the legitimate spiral ramp inside the same tile. BRM south-face has no equivalent legitimate alternate corridor inside (46,34); aggressive disconnection just pushes Detour to neighbouring tiles whose own cliffs are worse. Single-tile config is the wrong lever for this geometry; needs either coordinated multi-tile bake (tighter walkableClimb across 46,28..36 simultaneously) or a different bake parameter (for example walkableHeight or filterLedgeSpans tuning) that fragments cliff-edge cells without destroying horizontal connectivity. See memory project_pfs_overhaul_006_pathphysicsprobe_brm_route.md for the probe-driven evidence and project_pfs_overhaul_006_brm_singletile_negative.md for this round.
0._3446_README_erosion: 2026-05-13: try the OG-style source-support erosion split without changing Detour/header capsule or climb. walkableErosionRadius=0.2y trims rcErodeWalkableArea only; it is meant to preserve thin WMO/source surfaces for the BRD run while avoiding the earlier climb-fragmentation regression.
0._3546_README_erosion: 2026-05-13: live Flame Crest -> BRD/UBRS/LBRS/BWL tests stall around (-7519,-2100,130), which maps to MmapGen tile 35,46 / runtime 0004635.mmtile. Apply the same source-support erosion split and export a focused stall crop before investigating BotRunner execution.
0._3546_README_slope_negative_iter2: 2026-05-13 (terrain-only retry, REVERTED again): a follow-up attempt set walkableSlopeAngle=52 on terrain (vmap left at default 61) to address the FG run regression from the both-sides-52 attempt. Live result: the bot escaped the original stall coord (-7519,-2100,130) but progressed only as far as (-7665,-1808,137) in Ruins of Thaurissan, where it pressed nose-first into a BRM rock wall while the pathfinder service returned route=none for replan attempts (plan=33..37 all returned no route). The user-supplied screenshot showed the bot pinned against a near-vertical rock face. The stuck detector did NOT catch the stall because the bot crept ~0.25y/s along the wall and the existing SnapshotStallGuard threshold (1.5y over 45s) was defeated by that creep. Reverted again. Next iteration must either (a) implement a runtime NAV_STEEP_SLOPES exclude in Exports/Navigation/PathFinder.cpp::createFilter (vmangos's Map.cpp pattern) so the mesh stays as-is and player paths skip steep-slope polys regardless of the bake's classification — leaves models intact, requires Navigation.dll + Docker rebuild — or (b) add an off-mesh connection for the BRM ascent so Detour has an explicit route up the mountain. The bake-level slope tightening is the wrong fix surface; both tried versions broke something else.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT_surface_F: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface F (2026-05-14, REVERTED): tried per-tile maxSimplificationError=0.5 → 'Too many vertices' overbuild (Detour's 16-bit vertex limit); fell back to 1.2 → tile baked (3020100 → 3619812 bytes, +20%) and Walkable_AllFourPortals_HaveGroundPoly flipped GREEN (ubrs_portal poly captured the queried XY this time). BUT the bake's denser polygon graph (corridor poly count 287→355 on FC→UBRS) caused TWO regressions on previously-GREEN gates — Corridor_FcToUbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly and Corridor_FcToLbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly both ended at NEW polyIdxes that don't match the portal polys (the upper-cluster polygon IDs all shifted under the tighter simplification). On top of that, SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump hung >9min (vs <100ms baseline) — catastrophic A* perf explosion. Net 11/14 → 10/14 and a live-FG-fatal query-time regression. The polygon-footprint-short-of-queried-XY hypothesis was correct but maxSimplificationError is the wrong knob: making the bake denser shifts every polygon's ID and explodes A* without a path to the literal portal coord. Future attempts: try walkableErosionRadius=0.0 (eliminate erosion margin entirely so polygon footprints extend to the source walkable boundary) or pursue Surface G/H first; revisit F if those don't expose a different fix surface.
0._3446_NEGATIVE_RESULT_surface_G: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface G (2026-05-14, REVERTED): tried per-tile walkableErosionRadius=0.0 (vs 0.2 baseline) to eliminate rcErodeWalkableArea margin and extend polygon footprints toward source walkable boundary. Hypothesis was that erosion-only changes wouldn't split polys (stable polyIdxes). FALSIFIED — the 0.0 bake produced erosionRadiusCells=0 (vs 1 baseline) and tile grew 3020100 → 3729412 bytes (+23%, comparable to Surface F's 1.2). Property tests (prod-data): Walkable_AllFourPortals_HaveGroundPoly GREEN (side-effect, same as Surface F mse=1.2), but Corridor_FcToBwlPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly STILL RED (corridor 1 poly short — term=0x14F02470 vs portal=0x14F024F1, just different polyIdxes from baseline) AND Corridor_FcToUbrsPortal/FcToLbrsPortal_TerminatesAtPortalPoly BOTH REGRESSED (was GREEN, now ends at 0x14F047DB which doesn't match new UBRS/LBRS portal polys 0x14F046E8 / 0x14F041A8) AND SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump HUNG >9min (same A* explosion as Surface F). Net 11/14 → 9/14 + fatal A* perf regression. Root cause clarified: ANY bake-parameter change to tile 3446 that touches the walkable-cell map (mse, erosionRadius, ch, cs, walkableSlopeAngle...) shifts the entire polygon graph because rcBuildRegions/rcBuildContours/rcBuildPolyMesh are deterministic from the walkable input but sensitive to small perturbations. The BWL corridor 1-poly-short symptom is robust to bake-knob tightening on this tile — fundamental tile-3446 connectivity gap, not a bake-fidelity issue. Surface H targets a DIFFERENT tile (Ruins of Thaurissan ascending corridor) and may not have this problem. After F and G negative, the structural conclusion is that tile 3446's corridor-terminus failures need a different fix surface than tile-3446 bake knobs — candidates: off-mesh connections (would need Phase 4 of overhaul), or recognizing the BWL/UBRS/LBRS portal polys have intentional in-game connectivity gaps that the bake cannot recover from generic terrain processing.
0._3546_README_surface_H: PFS-OVERHAUL BRM ascent Phase 2 Surface H (2026-05-14): tighten per-tile cs from default 0.2666 to 0.15 to fragment the 2.61y-dz polygon at WP[259] (-7673.9,-1746.2,136.0)→(-7679.0,-1747.5,133.4) into shorter steps. Per the cs+tileSize coupling rule (memory project_pfs_overhaul_006_config_key_inversion): when cs shrinks, tileSize must grow so 25*tileSize*cs ≈ 533.33y. For cs=0.15: tileSize = 533.33/(25*0.15) ≈ 142.22 → use 142. OG zeppelin precedent: cs=0.1 paired with tileSize=213 (25*213*0.1 = 532.5y). Target gate: SmoothPath_FcToUbrsPortal_NoUnreasonableZJump (4 violations, worst 2.61y at idx 259). Tile 3546 is the Flame Crest stall tile, a DIFFERENT tile from F/G's 3446 — so the tile-3446 corridor-terminus tests won't see polyIdx churn.

1._4029_README: Per-tile override for the Orgrimmar zeppelin tower tile. The vmangos tile frame is swapped relative to normal WoW XYZ: tileX is derived from world Y and tileY is derived from world X. CLI invocation is `MmapGen.exe 1 --tile 40,29` and writes `mmaps/0012940.mmtile` (`sprintf` order is mapId, tileY, tileX). `TileWorker::getTileConfig` looks up the nested key as `tileX || tileY`, so the live OG dock key is `"4029"`. The older `2940`/`0014029` notes were wrong-tile artifacts.
1._4029_README_REVALIDATE: These knobs were originally discovered while the visualization/docs had the tile axes reversed. They are carried forward to the corrected Orgrimmar tile because the design intent still belongs here, but the next regen must revalidate them against fresh 40,29 geometry before treating them as proven.
1._4029_README_cs: cs=0.1m (vs 0.2666m default) for fine horizontal resolution on thin overhanging structures (the deck edge wall + railing). Tile size grows with cs=0.1, but only for this one tile.
1._4029_README_ledgefilter: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 / Phase 6 per-tile ledge-filter overrides. treatOobNeighborAsCliff=false: dock cells whose voxel-column neighbors are out-of-tile-bounds (OG harbor edge) stop counting OOB as an infinite drop, so the ledge filter does not reject them. mixedAreaUsesTerrainClimb=true: dock-edge cells transitioning to model decorations on top keep using the 1.8y terrain climb instead of the tighter about-1.2y model-transition climb. Both flags default to legacy behavior in getDefaultConfig() so non-opted tiles are unaffected. See TileWorker.cpp::filterLedgeSpans.
1._4029_README_climb: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 Cycle 17 / Intervention 3 intended to disconnect the polygon corridor's lower-platform -> upper-deck shortcut at the OG zeppelin tower deck-lip. Default walkableClimbTerrain=1.8y is the harvested-from-client baseline; here we tighten to 0.2y for tile (40,29) ONLY. Revalidate with corrected 40,29 source geometry before promoting this to a closed fix.
1._4029_README_debug: debugStageCropWow is a focused MmapGen debug export crop for the Orgrimmar tower top ramp/deck. It writes stable heightfield, compact heightfield, and contour CSV stage files only while --debug is used.
1._4029_README_debug_multi: 2026-05-22: debugStageCropsWow adds extra focused windows without losing the original top-deck crop. Crop 0 stays the tower top-ramp/deck slice; the extra crop covers the city/hallway/exterior dead-end anchor cluster so compact-span region survival can be inspected before contours/polymesh generation.
1._4029_README_polys: maxVertsPerPoly=3 is a focused bake-quality probe/fix for the tower top-ramp/deck crop. The previous generator ignored this key; TileWorker now honors it so the OG deck can be tested without route-time repair.
1._4029_README_erosion: walkableErosionRadius=0.2y separates WMO floor-support erosion from the Tauren collision capsule. The Detour tile still advertises agentRadius=1.0247y / agentHeight=2.625y, but rcErodeWalkableArea no longer erases thin top-deck connector slabs that FG screenshots show characters can stand on. A 0.0y probe overbuilt the tile and exceeded Detour's 16-bit vertex limit.
1._4029_README_detour_trim: 2026-05-21: after the steep/mixed-wall Detour cull landed, the remaining deck-layer leak was a chain of tiny lower trim ledges shadowed directly under the upper deck. postDetourCullShadowedLedges=true enables a final-tile trim for those micro-ledges on this tile only; it is geometry-based (overlap + vertical shadow), not route-coordinate based.
1._4029_README_offmesh_trim: 2026-05-22/23: the raw-Detour cutover exposed a second OG tower defect: some authored boarding/underpass off-mesh starts were snapping onto steep trim chains instead of the broader lower approach support. postDetourCullOffMeshAnchorSteepTrim=true trims only steep candidate components that are actually selected from an authored off-mesh start landing poly on this tile. Leave the threshold keys at defaults unless a probe-backed experiment closes the route set without broad regressions.
1._4029_NEGATIVE_RESULT_offmesh_trim_widen: 2026-05-23 (REVERTED): widened the 40,29 off-mesh steep-trim thresholds to maxEdge2D=8.0, maxArea2D=16.0, maxZRange=4.25, componentMaxArea2D=32.0, componentMaxZRange=6.5 so landing poly 18421's underpass component would cull 13 members. The focused OG deck/route checks stayed green 6/6, but the broader raw-detour CriticalWalkLegs sweep cratered from 17/23 to 6/23 and regressed many unrelated Orgrimmar routes, including the main flight-master -> zeppelin corridor. Conclusion: the underpass leak does live in that off-mesh steep-trim component, but widening the component budget is too blunt and destroys legitimate lower-approach connectivity. Keep the per-tile override capability in code, but do not enable these expanded thresholds in config.
1._4029_README_shadowed_pockets: 2026-05-22: the remaining OG city/hallway dead-end reds probe into tiny walkable pocket components that overlap a higher competing ground layer inside the same XY footprint. postDetourCullShadowedPockets=true adds a conservative final-tile trim for those small vertically-shadowed pocket components on 40,29 only. This is intended to retarget start/corridor resolution away from stacked dead-end slabs without changing global Recast knobs.
1._4029_README_anchor_poly_stacks: 2026-05-22/23: follow-up to the raw-Detour cutover. Probe coordinates proved that several remaining OG city/hallway/exterior reds were not smooth-path corner clipping but overlapping final Detour ground stacks at the same XY where findNearestPoly/corridor resolution could land on a dead-end competitor. postDetourCullAnchorPolyStacks=true uses a small list of verified anchor coords to keep the good support polys and disable only the competing stack members during bake. This is intentionally bake-side and tile-local; route-time repair is an anti-pattern.
1._4029_README_anchor_compact_stacks: 2026-05-23: stage dumps proved the remaining OG dead-end stack artifacts already exist at buildCHF/median time, before regions and contours. preRegionCullAnchorUpperCompactSpans=true nulls only the immediate upper stacked compact spans in verified anchor cells so those bogus slabs never become regions/contours/polys.
1._4029_README_anchor_source_support: 2026-05-23: source-geometry-backed follow-up to the anchor compact-span cull. preMedianCullAnchorSourceSupportCompetingSpans=true lets the median filter see a cleaned source-backed support surface instead of the contaminated lower basin, while preRegionCullAnchorSourceSupportCompetingSpans=true repeats the cleanup before regions. preRegionCullAnchorSourceSupportFallbackToWindow=true extends that proof-backed cull to nearby no-support cells inside the same anchor window so restored upper support does not leave the lower basin owning adjacent cells. This uses richer WoW input directly instead of trusting the already-quantized compact stack to choose the right floor.
1._4029_README_anchor_support_restore: 2026-05-23: preRegionRestoreAnchorSourceSupportAfterErode=true is a focused compact-heightfield preservation experiment for the hallway/city dead-end anchors. It restores only source-backed support-band spans that were walkable before rcErodeWalkableArea and got erased by erosion, before median/regions run. Combined with the source-support cull it improved several anchor-stage answers without regressing the focused 7/7 slice, but the full raw-Detour route sweep still stayed 17/23 so future work should probe the shifted dead-end coords instead of treating this as a complete fix.
1._4029_README_pre_region_anchor_coords: 2026-05-24: preRegionAnchorCoordsWow decouples the source-support / upper-compact cleanup window from the final-Detour anchor stack cull list. Use it when a hallway dead-end coord needs earlier compact/source cleanup proof without also enabling postDetour stack trimming at that same XY. If omitted, the compact/source cleanup still falls back to postDetourCullAnchorPolyStacksCoordsWow for backward compatibility.
1._4029_README_source_support_borrow: 2026-05-24: borrowMissingAnchorSourceSupportFromNeighbors is an experiment-only manifest/source-support fallback. It lets a missing anchor borrow the nearest same-band source support from a nearby anchor, but it is disabled by default because the first hallway experiment fixed the 1521.3 sourceSupport blind spot without improving the full 17/23 route sweep.
1._4029_README_anchor_stage_manifest: 2026-05-23: writeAnchorStageManifest=true is the preferred stage-proof surface for the remaining Orgrimmar stacked-support defects. One bake now writes a machine-readable per-anchor stage manifest under meshes/, and bake-tile.ps1 copies it into tmp/bake-sweeps/<variant>/analysis/ plus a NavDataAudit summary JSON/CSV. Optional anchorStageManifestCoordsWow adds analysis-only probe coords to the manifest without changing the actual compact-span or final-Detour cull coord list. Use this to answer 'first bad stage = X' before changing bake knobs again.
1._4029_README_anchor_stage_logs: 2026-05-23: logAnchorStageDiagnostics is intentionally false by default. The old HF-SRC-ANCHOR / CHF-SRC-ANCHOR / CHF-SRC-COMP probe prints are still available for deep forensics, but they are noisy per-subtile diagnostics and should stay opt-in now that the structured manifest exists.
1._4029_README_anchor_probe_candidates: 2026-05-23: extra 40,29 anchor-cull probe coords from the actual city/hallway/hall-exit dead-end collapse points. These are intentionally tile-local and temporary until the logs prove which XYs can see the intended upper support layer versus which are just seam-noisy starts.
1._4029_README_anchor_routeability: 2026-05-24: anchorRouteTargetsWow gives the finalDetour manifest a routeability proof surface for trapped local basins. postDetourCullAnchorTrappedComponents exists for focused experiments only: it disables a non-routeable local winner/component only when the same anchor window still has another support component that can route to the configured target. On tile 40,29 the shipped learning is that this proof surface is useful, but the cull is not promotable yet; keep it off by default until a branch improves real route results, not just component evidence.
1._4029_README_pre_poly_preserve: 2026-05-24: prePolyPreserveAnchorSupportCoordsWow is a targeted rcBuildPolyMesh border-vertex preservation experiment. Use it only for anchors proven to survive contours and then lose their source-backed support during polyMesh border-vertex removal. It preserves only RC_BORDER_VERTEX points on support-band contours for those anchors before rcBuildPolyMesh runs.
1._4029_README_pre_poly_raw: 2026-05-24: prePolyUseRawAnchorSupportContoursWow is a stronger contour-to-polymesh preservation experiment. It swaps a source-backed support contour from its simplified contour vertices back to its raw contour vertices immediately before rcBuildPolyMesh, so we can test whether contour simplification is what erases a bad hallway support floor.
1._4029_README_pre_raster_source_promote: 2026-05-26 EXPERIMENT ONLY: preRasterizePromoteAnchorSourceSupportCoordsWow plus preRasterizePromoteAnchorSourceSupportCorridorHalfWidth promotes real local source triangles from the recovered support->anchor corridor into the walkable raster input before the normal Recast stages run. Use it when tiny synthetic raster patches are exhausted and the next bounded retry needs a more structural source/raster shape without jumping back to generic knob churn.
1._4029_README_pre_poly_band_local: 2026-05-25 EXPERIMENT ONLY: prePolyResimplifyAnchorSupportBandLocalPreserveRadius reinjects only the raw contour verts that stay inside the support band and a local anchor window. Use it when boundary-only carry is too sparse and full local-raw carry is too broad. Keep it absent from the checked-in 4029 config unless a branch improves real route results, not just local contour shape.
1._4029_EXPERIMENT_pre_poly_raw_preserve_c: 2026-05-24 (EXPERIMENT ONLY): enabling both prePolyUseRawAnchorSupportContoursWow and prePolyPreserveAnchorSupportCoordsWow for anchor 1523.8 produced hash 52D99D419A201AC86DA1512A1BBDAFC0F955627B11A0A96041732DCD22DF2FC8, kept the focused OG slice green (7/7), and moved 1523.8 from polymesh/upper_support_lost to finalDetour/lower_competitor_dominant. Full raw-Detour CriticalWalkLegs still stayed 17/23 because the surviving support contour fragmented into non-routeable final support shards. Keep both keys absent from the checked-in config until a branch improves the real route set, not just the stage answer.
1._4029_NEGATIVE_RESULT_pre_poly_maxverts4: 2026-05-24 (REJECTED): maxVertsPerPoly=4 on top of the raw+preserve 1523.8 contour branch produced hash 6530FC7C41C030557088AFED612BE667BB279F4BECB667F00C60CAB15E07F9C1 and made several hallway/exterior anchors look more routeable in the manifest, but it regressed the focused OG slice to 5/7 by reintroducing a giant bridge polygon and losing the intended deck connector footprint. Treat maxVertsPerPoly=4 the same way as =6 on tile 40,29: not promotable.
1._4029_NEGATIVE_RESULT_anchor_routeabilit: 2026-05-24 (DISABLED BY DEFAULT): two routeability-aware finalDetour cull variants were validated on tile 40,29. og_4029_anchor_routeability_cull saved hash B84D1CD2369E03721ECBDC83656EC4E700E546886CFF49C231F52F05CED086AF and og_4029_anchor_routeability_chain_targets saved hash 039BEDF73A2318B0D6559BDC0FB453D240875EDD08BA2319F56A0EA26D85EA94. Both kept the focused OG slice green (7/7) but the full raw-Detour CriticalWalkLegs sweep stayed 17/23. Practical proof: city/hallway/hall-exit anchors still resolved zero routeable support components, so the cull could not close those trapped basins; underpass/exterior anchors did become routeable, but the underpass route regressed into an overhead-ramp/ceiling climb instead of the desired lower escape. Keep the routeability manifest fields; do not leave the trapped-component cull enabled in the default tile config yet.
1._4029_NEGATIVE_RESULT_partition_layers_s: 2026-05-23 (REVERTED): isolated the active 40,29 `partitionType` / `maxSimplificationError` knobs after the source-backed tile regressed. Three focused experiment bakes proved the problem is not just 'layers is newer': (A) `watershed + maxSimplificationError=1.3` produced hash 932A176CD19C96B38E319ACDFD085A3BD9BC68E00FB6A792AB541F69F7AC713C and failed 3/7 focused OG gates, including giant bridge polys and loss of the off-mesh Frezza boarding leg (`offMeshPolyCount=0`); (B) `layers + 1.8` produced hash 814BA912D2089383FEB6AA5836AC4FAC62F16FE21B22E9B2FEE8DD2E2B2DBBE3 and still failed 2/7 focused OG gates (shadowed lower trim ledge + lost deck connector density); (C) removing both overrides restored the source-backed watershed/default-simplification tile hash FB2FBAF1848FC2ACFB1F9E093A8EC99284C9C19843CD64E4F15CA4FBBF3315D6 and returned the focused slice to 7/7 while the broader raw-Detour sweep returned to the known 17/23 baseline. Practical rule: do not tighten `maxSimplificationError` on 40,29 as a geometry-quality fix; it over-densifies contours enough to drop off-mesh connectivity and reintroduce giant bridge polys. Keep `layers` experimental-only until a bake-side trim closes its remaining deck regressions.
1._4029_NEGATIVE_RESULT_loop24_A3: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 loop-24 Phase A3 (2026-05-18, REVERTED): single coordinated 2-knob delta calibrated from loop-24 Phase A2 stack data. Knob A walkableErosionRadius 0.2 -> 0.3 + Knob B walkableHeight 0 (auto=11) -> 14 (=3.5y). Tile baked clean (md5 fbe57ed439986b87c5613edf9bc39a59, size 8070292 = -8.4% from 8805608 baseline). Probe at coord 2 (1350.2,-4528.6,34.0): phantom stack count UNCHANGED at 63 phantoms + 1 legit ground (polyref 281475331146733, surfaceZ=37.509, posOverPoly=1 — polyIdx shifted 18398 -> 17389 but coord 2's structural trap is identical). Probe at coords 1 + 3: revealed a SEPARATE catastrophic regression — MmapGen ran without --offMeshInput so the existing OG-zeppelin off-mesh connections were dropped (coord 1: 1 poly -> 0, coord 3: 5 polys -> 4). Even with offmesh.txt threaded correctly, the unchanged phantom stack means knobs A+B don't close the trap. Reverted prod-data + config before live test cycle. Forbidden knobs respected. Conclusion: these two knobs are not effective on the (40,29) phantom stack class; the phantoms are not narrow-step / low-clearance cells. Advancing to Phase A4 (validator-driven targeted cull) which IS the structural fit for coord 2 (only-coord-with-cull-viable per Phase A2 architectural refinement).
1._3928_README_doodad_gap: loop-25 Phase C1 (2026-05-19): debug-only debugStageCropWow over the ClimbOrgrimmarZeppelinTowerRampToFrezza stall column. Two standalone M2 doodad instances 224791+224792 form a ~4.6y vertical-wall pair at approx (1615.3, -4240.85), z=46.66..51.30. Bot stalls at (1616.7,-4242.4,46.7) on the next walk segment toward (1614.7,-4242.1,46.7). The crop covers WoW (1614..1618, -4243..-4239) horizontally and z=46..52 vertically — sufficient to capture wall voxels at every Recast stage. No bake-parameter changes here; this is investigation-only and is expected to leave the mmtile bits byte-identical.
